title: AFI Provider Instance v1 (FACTORY-CONTRACT — tenant-scoped, non-secret provider configuration)
description: A tenant- or operator-scoped, non-secret configuration binding one provider to one trusted registered adapter for exactly one category (afi.provider-instance.v1). A ProviderInstance is what a pipeline node references (by id + record version) to select a configured provider for its category. It identifies exactly one category, exactly one provider, and one registered adapter implementation (id + version); it is version-pinned for deterministic composition; it OPTIONALLY references one compatible CredentialRef (by opaque id — never a value); it carries only non-secret invocation settings (a closed object; no arbitrary endpoint URL, anti-SSRF); and it fails validation when provider, category, adapter, or credential requirements conflict. It contains NO credential value, NO arbitrary executable code, and NO analyst-supplied remote endpoint. This is a fixture/reference shape only — deployment-specific instance records are tenant configuration, not a public registry (PBF-GOV D-PBF-7).
$comment: BOUNDARY (FACTORY-CONTRACT): schema/contract only — NO credential resolution, NO provider invocation, NO endpoint. The pipeline node carries only a versioned reference to this record; the runtime provider-adapter layer (afi-reactor, BELOW the node) resolves the instance, validates compatibility, resolves ONLY the authorized credential via a least-privilege SecretResolver, and invokes the registered adapter. invocation.endpointProfile is a named, allow-listed profile resolved by the adapter/deployment — never an analyst-supplied raw URL.
x-afiPartOf: AFI Provider Adapter & BYOK Foundations v0.1 (PBF-GOV)
x-afiDoctrineRefs: afi-governance/decisions/provider-byok-foundations-v0.1 (PBF-GOV) D-PBF-4/D-PBF-5/D-PBF-7/D-PBF-8 — ProviderInstance is tenant/operator-scoped and non-secret; one category, one provider, one registered adapter; references (never embeds) a credential; runtime resolution is least-privilege
afi-governance/decisions/factory-configurable-pipelines-v1 (FCP-GOV) D-FCP-1 — the five open lanes; a node binds a plugin/provider of its own category

x-afiConstraints.noSecretMaterial: PRIMARY: additionalProperties:false at every object level (record and invocation) plus explicit allowed fields — an inline apiKey/token/secret/password/authorization field has nowhere to live. SECONDARY: the BYOK denylist validator rejects secret-named fields. credentialRef is an OPAQUE id pointer, never a value.
x-afiConstraints.oneCategoryOneProviderOneAdapter: An instance identifies exactly one category, exactly one provider (providerId), and one registered adapter (adapterId + adapterVersion). Cross-field rules (provider exists; category is a member of the provider's supportedCategories; adapterId equals the provider's adapterId; adapterVersion is registered) are enforced by the runtime adapter registry (afi-reactor) and the provider-BYOK cross-reference tests.
x-afiConstraints.credentialCoherence: CROSS-FIELD: credentialRef is present IFF the bound provider requiresCredential. A credentialed provider without a credentialRef fails closed; a keyless provider with a credentialRef is rejected (unauthorized credential). When present, the referenced afi.credential-ref.v1 MUST be active, tenant-scoped to this instance's tenant, and compatible with this provider (providerId + credentialKind).
x-afiConstraints.noArbitraryEndpoint: There is no raw endpoint/URL field. invocation.endpointProfile is a named, allow-listed profile only — an analyst cannot supply an arbitrary remote endpoint (anti-SSRF).
x-afiConstraints.modelAuthority: When model is present, it MUST be a member of the bound provider's supportedModels (cross-field, enforced by the cross-reference tests).

schema.description: Schema-id version of the provider instance.

providerInstanceId.description: Stable id of this instance. A pipeline node references it by (providerInstanceId, recordVersion).

recordVersion.description: Semantic version of THIS instance record (no v prefix), version-pinned for deterministic composition.

tenant.description: Tenant/operator scope that owns this instance. The runtime resolves only credentials scoped to this tenant (least-privilege, D-PBF-7).

category.description: The single open analysis lane this instance supplies (never 'merge' or 'scorer'). MUST equal the referencing node's category and be a member of the provider's supportedCategories.

providerId.description: The single provider (afi.provider.v1 providerId) this instance configures.

adapterId.description: The trusted, build-time-registered adapter that executes this instance (afi-reactor adapter registry). MUST equal the provider's adapterId. NEVER a filesystem path or code URL.

adapterVersion.description: Exact semver of the bound adapter (no v prefix), pinned for deterministic composition.

model.description: OPTIONAL model/algorithm identity; when present MUST be a member of the provider's supportedModels (x-afiConstraints.modelAuthority).

credentialRef.description: OPTIONAL opaque reference to a compatible afi.credential-ref.v1 (by its credentialRef id). NEVER a secret value. Present IFF the bound provider requiresCredential (x-afiConstraints.credentialCoherence).

invocation.description: OPTIONAL non-secret invocation settings. Closed object — no secret fields, no arbitrary endpoint URL.

windowHours.description: OPTIONAL lookback window in hours (non-secret tuning).

timeoutMs.description: OPTIONAL per-invocation timeout in milliseconds (non-secret tuning).

endpointProfile.description: OPTIONAL named, allow-listed endpoint profile resolved by the adapter/deployment — NEVER an analyst-supplied raw URL (x-afiConstraints.noArbitraryEndpoint).

status.description: Instance status. Only 'active' instances are selectable; 'inactive' retires without deletion.
